question: Quel niveau d'autonomie accorder a un agent de codage IA par type d'action (read, edit, commit, push, deploy) ?

principles.text: Utiliser une autonomie graduee par type d'action : read-only → edit-in-branch → commit → push → deploy, avec approbation humaine explicite entre tiers. Ne jamais utiliser le mode 'bypassPermissions' ou equivalent YOLO sur des repos production. L'autonomie doit etre commensurate avec le risque et le blast radius de l'action.

sources.name: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (AI Act) Article 14 Human Oversight
sources.quote: The oversight measures shall be commensurate with the risks, level of autonomy and context of use of the high-risk AI system

sources.name: Parasuraman, Sheridan & Wickens 2000 - A Model for Types and Levels of Human Interaction with Automation
sources.quote: automation can be applied to four broad classes of functions: 1) information acquisition; 2) information analysis; 3) decision and action selection; and 4) action implementation

sources.name: Claude Code permission-modes documentation
sources.quote: Each mode makes a different tradeoff between convenience and oversight

sources.name: Faros AI - DORA Report 2025 Key Takeaways
sources.quote: Incidents per PR are up 242.7%, meaning for every code change merged, the probability of a production incident has more than tripled

sources.name: Toth, Feng et al. arXiv:2509.14745 - Empirical Study of Agentic Coding PRs
sources.quote: 567 pull requests across 157 open-source projects; 83.77% of agentic-generated PRs were accepted and merged

etd_balance.benefices: Controle fin (conformite EU AI Act Art 14), prevention d'incidents destructifs, calibration de la confiance
etd_balance.risques: Friction cognitive (approval fatigue), ralentissement flow, contournement via 'always allow'
